name: Mezmo (LogDNA) API
what_it_does: Cloud log management and observability pipeline platform with REST API for aggregating, parsing, routing, and analyzing logs from any source at scale (formerly LogDNA).
use_cases: Querying application and infrastructure logs programmatically for incident investigation agents
Creating log alerts and setting up monitoring rules via API in automated deployments
Building log transformation pipelines that route and enrich data via Mezmo Pipeline
Exporting log data for compliance archival or secondary analysis
Correlating log patterns with alerts for automated root cause analysis
not_for: Real-time metrics and tracing without log co-location (use Datadog or Grafana for full stack)
Ultra-low latency log streaming requiring sub-second query response
Organizations needing self-hosted log management for data sovereignty
best_when: An agent needs to query, analyze, and alert on logs across cloud-native applications without managing logging infrastructure.
avoid_when: You need full observability (logs + metrics + traces) from a single vendor — Mezmo is log-focused.

auth.notes: Service key or ingestion key from Mezmo account. Ingestion key for sending logs; API key for reading. Keys available from account settings. No scope granularity beyond read/write split.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free tier: 100MB/day log ingestion with 0-day search retention
pricing.paid_tiers: Pay-per-use from ~$1.50/GB after free; Team plans from $99/month
pricing.notes: Mezmo offers flexible pay-as-you-go or committed plans. IBM Cloud integration available (IBM acquired LogDNA in 2021). Mezmo Pipeline product adds stream processing on top of logs.

performance.rate_limits: Search API: 100 req/min; Ingestion API: unlimited burst

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API documentation covers core operations. Error responses are JSON with status codes and messages. Log search API supports timeframe filtering and query syntax.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Log ingestion is append-only and naturally idempotent from the query perspective. Alert configuration supports idempotent updates.
known_agent_gotchas: Log retention period limits how far back agents can query — varies by plan
Search queries use Mezmo's query syntax (similar to Elasticsearch but not identical)
Streaming log export via WebSocket requires different connection setup than REST API
IBM acquisition has led to some documentation fragmentation between Mezmo and IBM Log Analysis

security_score_components.security_notes: SOC2 Type II and HIPAA compliant. GDPR EU data residency option. TLS enforced. Single API key model without scopes is a limitation for multi-tenant agent deployments.
